skill.name: nano-banana-2
skill.description: Generate images with Google Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview (Nano Banana 2) via inference.sh CLI. Capabilities: text-to-image, image editing, multi-image input (up to 14 images), Google Search grounding. Triggers: nano banana 2, nanobanana 2, gemini 3.1 flash image, gemini 3 1 flash image preview, google image generation
skill.summary: Generate images with Google Gemini 3.1 Flash via inference.sh CLI with text-to-image, image editing, and Google Search grounding
skill.author: tul-sh

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The skill is a documentation wrapper for inference.sh CLI to generate images using Google Gemini 3.1. The backticks flagged are markdown code fences, not Ruby execution. URLs point to legitimate inference.sh documentation. No cryptographic code exists.

high_findings.title: External Command Execution (False Positive)
high_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged backtick syntax as Ruby execution. These are markdown code fences (```bash) in documentation examples, not actual code execution.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Backticks in SKILL.md are markdown code block delimiters, not Ruby backtick operators. No actual command execution occurs - this is documentation only.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (False Positive)
high_findings.description: Static analyzer incorrectly flagged text patterns as cryptographic code. This skill contains no cryptographic algorithms.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: This is a pure image generation documentation file. No cryptographic code or algorithms exist. The static analyzer appears to have misidentified text patterns.

high_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs (False Positive)
high_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged hardcoded URLs. These are legitimate documentation links to inference.sh and example image URLs.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs point to inference.sh official documentation and example image placeholders. No sensitive data exfiltration or malicious network behavior.

content.value_statement: Create stunning images using Google Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview through inference.sh CLI. Supports text-to-image, image editing with up to 14 input images, custom aspect ratios, resolutions up to 4K, and Google Search grounding for real-time information.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text prompts using Google Gemini 3.1 Flash
Edit existing images by providing up to 14 input images with modification prompts
Control output with aspect ratios (1:1, 16:9, 9:16, 4:3, 3:4, auto)
Set resolution from 1K to 4K for high-quality output
Enable Google Search grounding for real-time information in generated images
Generate multiple images (up to 4) in a single request
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI (infsh) to be installed and configured
User must have an inference.sh account with API access
Image editing requires valid image URLs or local file paths
Google Search grounding requires network connectivity and may have rate limits

use_cases.title: Quick Image Generation
use_cases.description: Generate single images from text prompts for content creation, social media, or prototyping
use_cases.target_user: Content creators, marketers, developers needing quick visual assets

use_cases.title: Image Editing and Variation
use_cases.description: Edit existing images or generate variations by providing input images with modification prompts

use_cases.title: Research and Visualization
use_cases.description: Use Google Search grounding to visualize current events, weather, or real-time data

prompt_templates.title: Basic Text-to-Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the nano-banana-2 skill to generate an image of a futuristic cityscape at sunset with flying cars
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate a simple image from a text description

prompt_templates.title: Custom Style and Aspect Ratio
prompt_templates.prompt: Use nano-banana-2 to create a minimalist logo design for a coffee shop in vector style with 1:1 aspect ratio
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate an image with specific style and dimension requirements

prompt_templates.title: Image Editing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use nano-banana-2 to edit the image at https://example.com/landscape.jpg by adding a rainbow in the sky
prompt_templates.scenario: Modify an existing image with specific instructions

prompt_templates.title: High-Resolution with Grounding
prompt_templates.prompt: Use nano-banana-2 to generate a 4K resolution illustration of the current weather in Tokyo visualized as an artistic scene with Google Search enabled
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate high-quality images with real-time data integration

output_examples.input: Generate an image of a banana in space, photorealistic
output_examples.output: Returns generated image URL(s) with description metadata showing the AI-generated banana floating in space with realistic lighting and texture

output_examples.input: Generate 4 minimalist logo designs for a coffee shop with 1:1 aspect ratio
output_examples.output: Returns 4 different logo variations in square format, each with unique design elements suitable for a coffee brand

best_practices: Be specific and detailed in prompts to get better results - include style, lighting, composition, and mood details
Use appropriate aspect ratios for your use case (1:1 for social media, 16:9 for wide banners, 9:16 for stories)
Start with lower resolutions (1K) for faster iteration, then upgrade to 4K for final output
anti_patterns: Avoid vague prompts like 'make something cool' - be specific about subject, style, and mood
Do not provide more than 14 input images for editing as this will fail
Avoid using enable_google_search for purely fictional subjects as it may conflict with the prompt intent

faq.question: What is inference.sh and do I need an account?
faq.answer: Yes, inference.sh is the platform that provides access to Gemini models. You need to create an account and configure the CLI with your credentials.

faq.question: Can I edit multiple images at once?
faq.answer: Yes, you can provide up to 14 input images for editing in a single request using the images array parameter.

faq.question: What resolution should I use?
faq.answer: Use 1K for quick previews and iteration. Use 4K only for final production assets as it takes longer to generate.

faq.question: How does Google Search grounding work?
faq.answer: When enabled, the model can access real-time information from Google Search to incorporate current data like weather, news, or events into your image.

faq.question: What image formats are supported for input?
faq.answer: Input images must be JPEG, PNG, or WebP format. Provide them as URLs or local file paths.

faq.question: Can I generate multiple images in one request?
faq.answer: Yes, set the num_images parameter to generate up to 4 images in a single request.
